title: Kerala Erissery

ingredients.name: pumpkin (red or yellow), peeled and cubed 2 cm

subs.name: raw banana (vazhakkai) cubed, for a traditional vanpayar-kaya erissery
subs.notes: no change in method; raw banana gives a starchier, denser dish

subs.name: red cowpeas (vanpayar) or moong dal
subs.notes: moong dal cooks faster — no soaking needed, 2 pressure cooker whistles

ingredients.name: fresh grated coconut
visual_checks: ½ cup for paste, ¼ cup for dry-roasted topping
ingredients.notes: ½ cup for paste, ¼ cup for dry-roasted topping

subs.name: any neutral oil, though the topping loses its characteristic flavour

steps.text: Pressure cook the soaked black-eyed peas in enough water to cover for 3 whistles until tender. Drain and set aside. If using canned peas, skip this step.
Grind half the grated coconut (about 6 tablespoons) with cumin seeds and a splash of water to a smooth, thick paste. Set aside.

Place the pumpkin cubes in a pan with turmeric, chilli powder, salt and just enough water to cover the base of the pan (about ½ cup). Cover and cook on medium flame for 8–10 minutes until the pumpkin is tender and most of the water has absorbed.

Add the cooked black-eyed peas and the coconut-cumin paste. Stir gently and cook uncovered on medium-low for 5 minutes, mashing a few pieces of pumpkin against the side of the pan to naturally thicken the dish.

In a separate small kadai, heat coconut oil on medium flame. Splutter the mustard seeds, add dried red chillies and 1 sprig of curry leaves. Then add the remaining ¼ cup grated coconut and roast, stirring constantly, for 3–4 minutes until the coconut is dark golden-brown and very fragrant. This is the critical topping — do not rush it.

Pour the roasted coconut and tempering over the erissery and fold it in gently, leaving some on top for texture. Add the second sprig of curry leaves, check salt, and serve with rice as part of an Onam feast.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: pumpkin → raw banana (vazhakkai) cubed, for a traditional vanpayar-kaya erissery (no change in method; raw banana gives a starchier, denser dish); lobia → red cowpeas (vanpayar) or moong dal (moong dal cooks faster — no soaking needed, 2 pressure cooker whistles); coconut-oil → any neutral oil, though the topping loses its characteristic flavour
